techniques.evidence_quote: A user also has reported unsuccessful attempts to use AWS credentials shared with Datadog.
We traced the intrusion to the leak of an AWS access key and a SSH private key that were used by our automated provisioning and release systems.
We have detected unauthorized activity associated with a handful of production infrastructure servers, including a database that stores user credentials.
By using those particular keys together the attacker gained unauthorized access to three of our AWS EC2 instances and a subset of our AWS S3 buckets.
By using those particular keys together the attacker gained unauthorized access to three of our AWS EC2 instances.
Those AWS resources included user credentials for the Datadog service, service metadata, and credentials shared with Datadog for third-party integrations.

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained a leaked AWS access key and SSH private key that were used by Datadog's automated CI/CD provisioning and release systems — the root cause of the compromise.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: We traced the intrusion to the leak of an AWS access key and a SSH private key that were used by our automated provisioning and release systems.

attack_chain.description: Using the leaked SSH private key together with the AWS access key, the attacker gained unauthorized SSH access to three Datadog EC2 production instances starting 2016-07-07 at 19:44 UTC.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: By using those particular keys together the attacker gained unauthorized access to three of our AWS EC2 instances.

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the AWS access key to enumerate accessible AWS resources, discovering EC2 instances and S3 buckets containing sensitive data.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: By using those particular keys together the attacker gained unauthorized access to three of our AWS EC2 instances and a subset of our AWS S3 buckets.

attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed a subset of S3 buckets containing Datadog user credentials, service metadata, and customer-shared third-party integration credentials (including AWS keys).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Those AWS resources included user credentials for the Datadog service, service metadata, and credentials shared with Datadog for third-party integrations.

attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed a production database storing Datadog user credentials (bcrypt-hashed passwords) and customer-shared credentials for third-party integrations.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: We have detected unauthorized activity associated with a handful of production infrastructure servers, including a database that stores user credentials.

attack_chain.description: Attacker attempted to use harvested customer AWS credentials (shared with Datadog for integrations) to pivot into customer AWS environments, but these attempts were unsuccessful.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: A user also has reported unsuccessful attempts to use AWS credentials shared with Datadog.

initial_access_vector: Leaked AWS access key and SSH private key from Datadog's CI/CD automated provisioning and release systems
privilege_escalation: Attacker used the combined AWS access key + SSH private key to access production EC2 instances and S3 buckets with the elevated privileges already associated with those CI/CD service keys
